rule: Dog selected or randomly assigned. Show avatar and input only. No opening dialogue or prompt chips.
Combine reaction, approved fact if needed, character observation, destination and optional animation cue.
reason: Creates variety without writing every full conversation separately.

rule: Do not repeat the exact line, destination or article within the same browser session where alternatives exist.
Use context first, then weighted choice between Play, Learn, Discover and Get 30% Off.
reason: Avoid predictable menus while maintaining commercial intent.

rule: Carry original input, detected topic, answered facts, active campaign state and previous dogs into the next operator.
reason: The new dog should respond to the subject, not greet from scratch.

rule: Repeated handoffs are allowed and can revisit a previous dog.
reason: The call-centre loop is part of the comedy.

rule: Approximately 20 human submissions across the entire chat.
reason: Technical protection only. Do not display a conventional limit warning.

rule: At the limit or suitable comic moment, transfer to Boxer. He claims he cut off the previous visitor and then closes this one.
Pause conversational routing and counters while an embedded mini-game runs.
reason: Moves and guesses are not chat turns.

rule: Visitor can close at any time. A new session resets used lines unless product analytics requires an anonymous session token.
PRE_LAUNCH, LIVE, SOLD_OUT or PAUSED changes commercial actions without rewriting dialogue data.
Never generate or guess an answer. Use a character limitation line and redirect or transfer.
